---
title: "batch_create_orders"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `batch_create_orders()`

Batch Create Orders

## Usage

```r
batch_create_orders(orders, kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API", demo = FALSE)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `orders` | List of order specifications. Each order should be a list with: ticker, side, action, count, type, and optionally yes_price/no_price. |
| `kalshi_access_token` | Character. Environment variable name for API key. |
| `demo` | Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE. |

## Value

A list with created orders and any errors.

## Description

Creates multiple orders in a single request. Limited to 20 orders per batch.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
orders <- list(
  list(
    ticker = "MARKET1", side = "yes", action = "buy",
    count = 10, type = "limit", yes_price = 45
  ),
  list(
    ticker = "MARKET2", side = "no", action = "buy",
    count = 5, type = "limit", no_price = 30
  )
)
result <- batch_create_orders(orders)

## End(Not run)
```

## See also

Other trading: 
`amend_order()`,
`batch_cancel_orders()`,
`cancel_all_orders()`,
`cancel_order()`,
`create_order()`,
`decrease_order()`,
`get_order()`
